mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.5%) signals maximum monetary accommodation. This regime produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity valuations expand under near-zero discount rates and liquidity abundance.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline and recession risk increases.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns exceeds -0.15 in absolute value.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth, equity earnings expectations rise, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism requires synchronized demand signals to activate.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both RSAFS and HOUST grow month-over-month, or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at or above trend (102.0+) indicates robust supply-side capacity utilization and pricing power for equities. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as strong production data supports earnings revisions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window, or correlation reverses to negative in more than 40% of rolling 6-month periods.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 3% combined with FEDFUNDS below 2% creates a negative real rate environment. This interaction mechanism signals monetary policy is accommodative relative to inflation, supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term's Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 50,000 net new jobs per month, signaling labor market weakness and reduced consumer income growth. This threshold mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings growth expectations contract.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of instances when PAYEMS growth falls below 50,000, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly shows the fundamental demand trajectory. When GDPC1 growth rate decelerates below 1.5% annualized, recession risk rises and S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over 60 trading days as equity risk premiums expand.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances when GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5% annualized, or correlation between GDPC1 growth and forward returns exceeds -0.10 in absolute value.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) decline combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals stagflation conditions. This interaction mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity valuations compress under simultaneous growth weakness and cost pressures.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < 0% AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.3%
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered stagflation instances, or the interaction term's Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.
